fields.helpText: A type of data that is recognized as such within the framework of scanning DHCP forms and which requires its own Paper Keyboard description.
The maximum number of characters that can be input for this data type.

Used to print the field. Fields for certain types of data, such as telephone numbers and Social Security numbers usually require certain formats, such as 092-52-0493 for an SSN. The PRINT FORMAT allows the required format to be specified.
This is used to determine how much space on the form to allocate for the  hand print.
This is the confidence level (as defined by Paper Keyboard) that should be applied to this data type.
Will be used as a default label.
The unit of measurement as defined by the PCE Generic Device Interface.
The type of vitals measurement - used only for the PCE GDI VITALS node.
The unit of measurement - used only for the PCE GDI VITALS node.

The input transform should change X into a value that can be passed to the PCE Generic Device Interface. X should be killed if it not be transformed. Note that the return value of X need not meet all of PCE's validation requirments - for example, X may be an inactive code.

fields.setOfCodes: a:ALPHA (any ASCII);d:DATE (MM/DD/YY);f:FLOATING POINT NUMBER;i:INTEGER;t:TIME(HHMM, VA FileMan format);
fields.helpText: These are the data types provided for by Paper Keyboard.  The ALPHA data type can be further described by a Paper Keyboard picture clause. The Integer data type does not apply if the field can contain spaces or punctuation other than a '-'.

Enter a picture clause specific to Paper Keyboard.  A picture clause describes the intended format for the data type.  This is similar to an M pattern match.  For example, a CPT code is 5 characters, the first character may be an alpha or numeric followed by 4 numeric characaters.  To represent this picture in Paper Keyboard, one would enter the picture X####.
Enter the name of a Paper Keyboard Dictionary.  A dictionary can be applied to ICR data types to improve the recognition of ICR fields.  This is the name of the file that Paper Keyboard uses.

When using print formats for paper keyboard you should enter the  picture clause in this field.  For example, the print format for a SSN might look like ___-__-____.  This would cause the dashes to be printed and user input to be accepted in three subfields with lenghts of 3, 2, and 4 numerics.  You would then enter the picture in this field as ###-##-####.  (note the # sign is used as the picture for numerics)
